message.content:
Read all specs in specs/ directory (001 through 008) and return a concise summary (max 500 tokens total) covering:
1. MMS purpose and core functionality
2. Key user-facing features
3. Main domain concepts (PM types, scheduling, optimization)
4. Integration points (Maximo, etc.)
5. UI capabilities (deck.gl, dashboards)

Focus on what end-users need to know, not implementation details.

1вҶ’# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2вҶ’# Part 1: Executive Summary & Decision Record
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’**Document Version:** 2.1  
     5вҶ’**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT  
     6вҶ’**Last Updated:** 2026-01-19
     7вҶ’
     8вҶ’---
     9вҶ’
    10вҶ’## 1.1 System Purpose
    11вҶ’
    12вҶ’The Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) is a constraint-based fleet management platform that optimizes train deployments and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year contract period. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with an integrated system providing:
    13вҶ’
    14вҶ’1. **Daily mileage tracking** with automated ingestion from CSV uploads
    15вҶ’2. **Run assignment optimization** balancing mileage across the fleet
    16вҶ’3. **Preventive maintenance scheduling** within resource constraints
    17вҶ’4. **Strategic planning** for 1-10 year horizons to prevent "bow wave" accumulation
    18вҶ’5. **Man-hour leveling** to optimize workforce utilization
    19вҶ’6. **Cost forecasting** with TCO projection and variance analysis
    20вҶ’7. **Simulation capabilities** for what-if scenario planning
    21вҶ’8. **Unified visualization** via an Infinite Canvas interface
    22вҶ’9. **Service demand validation** ensuring fleet availability meets operational requirements
    23вҶ’10. **Unavailability awareness** integrating corrective maintenance status for schedule validity
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’---
    26вҶ’
    27вҶ’## 1.2 Project Scope
    28вҶ’
    29вҶ’### 1.2.1 In Scope (MMS Manages)
    30вҶ’
    31вҶ’| Capability | Description |
    32вҶ’|------------|-------------|
    33вҶ’| Fleet roster management | Trains, cars, components with lifecycle tracking |
    34вҶ’| Preventive Maintenance scheduling | C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul planning with constraint validation |
    35вҶ’| Run assignment optimization | Daily route allocation with mileage balancing |
    36вҶ’| Mileage projection and tracking | Route-based utilization profiles, 12-year forecasting |
    37вҶ’| Constraint validation | Track capacity, labor, materials, unavailability |
    38вҶ’| Maximo integration | Bidirectional sync for PM work orders |
    39вҶ’| Multi-year capital planning | Bow-wave detection, cost smoothing |
    40вҶ’| Service demand validation | Ensuring minimum fleet availability targets |
    41вҶ’
    42вҶ’### 1.2.2 In Scope (MMS Must Receive/React To)
    43вҶ’
    44вҶ’| External Data | Source | Purpose |
    45вҶ’|---------------|--------|---------|
    46вҶ’| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook/polling | Block unavailable trains from scheduling |
    47вҶ’| Inventory snapshots | ERP/Maximo | Validate material constraints |
    48вҶ’| Service demand requirements | Operations team | Set minimum availability targets |
    49вҶ’
    50вҶ’### 1.2.3 Out of Scope (MMS Does Not Execute)
    51вҶ’
    52вҶ’| Capability | Owner System | MMS Interaction |
    53вҶ’|------------|--------------|-----------------|
    54вҶ’| Corrective maintenance work orders | Maximo CMMS | Receives status updates only |
    55вҶ’| Parts inventory management | ERP/Maximo | Consumes snapshots for constraint validation |
    56вҶ’| Operator scheduling | Workforce management | None |
    57вҶ’| Real-time GPS tracking | OCC systems | None |
    58вҶ’
    59вҶ’> **Critical Clarification:** "Out of Scope" means MMS does not *manage* these functions. However, MMS **must receive** corrective maintenance status to prevent scheduling conflicts. Without this visibility, every PM schedule is potentially invalid on the day of execution.
    60вҶ’
    61вҶ’---
    62вҶ’
    63вҶ’## 1.3 Key Architectural Decisions
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’| ID | Decision | Rationale | Alternatives Considered | Risk Mitigation |
    66вҶ’|----|----------|-----------|------------------------|-----------------|
    67вҶ’| ADR-001 | TypeScript-First Architecture | Team expertise, single language for 98% of codebase, strong typing | Java (enterprise), Python (data science) | Node.js fallback path documented |
    68вҶ’| ADR-002 | C++ Native Binary for Tactical Optimization | Container density (20-30 pods/node), <5ms cold start, OR-Tools native performance | WASM solver (limited), Pure TS (10x slower) | TypeScript fallback solver, Docker build pipeline |
    69вҶ’| ADR-003 | Dual-Horizon Planning (Strategic + Tactical) | RFQ requires 1-10 year planning; single solver cannot handle both granularities | Single solver (bow wave risk), Manual strategic planning | Constraint aggregation layer ensures consistency |
    70вҶ’| ADR-004 | Soft Constraints with Explainability | Operations cannot accept "INFEASIBLE"; must provide actionable least-bad solutions | Hard constraints only (operationally unacceptable) | Fallback greedy solver, explanation trace |
    71вҶ’| ADR-005 | Material Constraints in Optimizer | PM scheduling without parts availability is operationally useless | Labor-only optimization (cited in reviews as critical gap) | Inventory snapshot integration, reservation system |
    72вҶ’| ADR-006 | Baseline/Drift Architecture | Long-term plans cannot recalculate daily; planners need stable reference points | Continuous recalculation (jittery UX, trust erosion) | Published baselines, drift thresholds, dampening |
    73вҶ’| ADR-007 | Scenario-Based Simulation | RFQ requires what-if analysis; cannot store in single-reality schema | In-memory only (data loss), Full DB clone (storage bloat) | Event-sourced deltas, materialized comparison views |
    74вҶ’| ADR-008 | Infinite Canvas UI (deck.gl) | Semantic zoom from 12-year strategy to daily operations without context switching | Paginated tables (poor UX), Separate views (fragmented) | Progressive enhancement, tabular fallback |
    75вҶ’| ADR-009 | Explanation Trace in Optimizer Output | Operators must understand "why" to trust and adopt system recommendations | Black-box output (user rejection, manual overrides) | Factor-based explanations, human-readable descriptions |
    76вҶ’| ADR-010 | Audit Trail for All Schedule Changes | 12-year government contract requires full attribution and compliance evidence | Implicit logging only | Immutable history tables, user attribution |
    77вҶ’| **ADR-011** | **Unavailability Event Integration** | PM scheduling requires knowledge of trains blocked by corrective work; without this, schedules are invalid on day of execution | Ignore corrective (operationally invalid) | Maximo webhook integration; manual status update UI as fallback |
    78вҶ’| **ADR-012** | **Service Demand as Hard Constraint** | Optimizer must know minimum fleet availability target; cannot validate bow-wave risk without knowing "how many trains do we need?" | Assume 100% availability (unrealistic) | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown |
    79вҶ’| **ADR-013** | **Route-Based Utilization Profiles** | Mileage projection accuracy requires route-specific daily rates, not fleet averages; projection error compounds over 12-year horizon | Fleet-wide average (60,000 km error over 12 years) | Route utilization profiles table; manual override per train; explicit flags for unknown routes |
    80вҶ’| **ADR-014** | **PM History Assumptions for Cold Start** | Data migration must handle incomplete historical records with explicit confidence levels; system must not crash or produce invalid projections from missing data | Assume complete history (crash risk), Ignore missing (false alerts) | Assumption tracking table; inference rules; confidence scoring; manual review queue |
    81вҶ’
    82вҶ’---
    83вҶ’
    84вҶ’## 1.4 Risk Assessment & Mitigation
    85вҶ’
    86вҶ’| Risk ID | Risk Description | Probability | Impact | Mitigation Strategy | Owner |
    87вҶ’|---------|------------------|-------------|--------|---------------------|-------|
    88вҶ’| R-001 | C++ binary build complexity | Medium | High | Containerized build pipeline, pre-built binaries, TypeScript fallback | DevOps |
    89вҶ’| R-002 | Team TypeScript focus with C++ component | Low | Medium | C++ contractor with knowledge transfer, comprehensive integration tests | Tech Lead |
    90вҶ’| R-003 | Strategic/Tactical solver inconsistency | Medium | High | Constraint aggregation layer, cross-validation tests, unified constraint model | Architect |
    91вҶ’| R-004 | Material constraint data quality | High | High | Inventory snapshot validation, fallback to labor-only mode, data quality dashboard | Data Team |
    92вҶ’| R-005 | Operator distrust of optimizer | Medium | High | Explanation traces, gradual rollout, manual override capability, training program | Product |
    93вҶ’| R-006 | Long-term plan instability (jitter) | Medium | Medium | Baseline snapshots, drift thresholds, dampening in projections | Backend |
    94вҶ’| R-007 | deck.gl WebGL performance on low-end devices | Medium | Medium | Progressive enhancement, LOD rendering, tabular fallback | Frontend |
    95вҶ’| R-008 | Data migration quality issues | Medium | High | Parallel-run validation, automated reconciliation, rollback procedures | Data Team |
    96вҶ’| R-009 | Scenario storage scalability | Low | Medium | Event-sourced deltas vs. full clones, automatic scenario expiration | Backend |
    97вҶ’| R-010 | Maximo integration delays | Medium | Medium | CSV interim solution, shadow record system, dedicated integration team | Integration |
    98вҶ’| **R-011** | **No corrective maintenance visibility** | **High** | **Critical** | Unavailability event feed from Maximo; fallback to manual status update UI; alert when train scheduled without availability check | Integration |
    99вҶ’| **R-012** | **Service demand undefined** | **High** | **Critical** | Require service demand input during onboarding; default to 85% fleet availability if unknown; prominent warning in UI when using defaults | Product |
   100вҶ’| **R-013** | **PM history incomplete at migration** | **High** | **High** | Assumption tracking table (`pm_baseline_assumptions`); inference rules from higher-level PMs; confidence scoring; manual review queue for LOW confidence trains | Data Team |
   101вҶ’| **R-014** | **Route utilization unknown** | **Medium** | **High** | Default to fleet-wide average with explicit warning flag; allow manual override per train; route profile editor in admin UI | Product |
   102вҶ’
   103вҶ’---
   104вҶ’
   105вҶ’## 1.5 RFQ Compliance Matrix
   106вҶ’
   107вҶ’| RFQ Requirement | Status | Implementation Section | Notes |
   108вҶ’|-----------------|--------|----------------------|-------|
   109вҶ’| Fleet size (128 trains, expandable to 500+) | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 3: Database Schema | UUID-based, no hard limits |
   110вҶ’| Daily mileage ingestion | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Ingestion Service | File watcher with validation |
   111вҶ’| Run assignment optimization | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Run Assignment Optimizer | C++ CP-SAT with soft constraints |
   112вҶ’| PM scheduling (weekly/monthly) | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: PM Scheduler | 12-week tactical horizon |
   113вҶ’| Long-term planning (1-10 years) | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Genetic algorithm with constraint envelope |
   114вҶ’| Man-hour projection & leveling | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Man-hour Leveling Engine | Multi-strategy adjustments |
   115вҶ’| Spare parts availability | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 3: Material Constraints Schema | Inventory integration, reservation system |
   116вҶ’| Costing module (TCO, variance) | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Costing Service | Labor rates, material costs, shop rates |
   117вҶ’| Simulation engine | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Simulation Service | Scenario branching, comparison views |
   118вҶ’| Guidance and warnings | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Explanation Trace | Factor-based assignment explanations |
   119вҶ’| Maximo integration | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 7: Integration | Phased: CSV ГўвҖ вҖҷ API ГўвҖ вҖҷ Bidirectional |
   120вҶ’| Web-based UI (desktop focus) | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 6: User Interface | Infinite Canvas with semantic zoom |
   121вҶ’| Email notifications & reports | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 5: API Specification | Notification service, PDF/Excel export |
   122вҶ’| 12-year contract support | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Strategic Planner | Baseline/drift architecture |
   123вҶ’| Strategic simulation capability | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 4: Simulation Service | What-if scenarios with persistence |
   124вҶ’| Component-level tracking | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 3: Component Tracking Schema | Mileage follows components |
   125вҶ’| Data migration | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 8: Data Migration | ETL pipeline, validation suite |
   126вҶ’| Documentation deliverables | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 8: Documentation Plan | User manual, API reference, training |
   127вҶ’| **Service availability validation** | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 3: Service Demand Schema | Min train requirements by day type |
   128вҶ’| **Corrective maintenance awareness** | ГўЕ“вҖҰ | Part 7: CM Integration | Maximo unavailability feed |
   129вҶ’
   130вҶ’---
   131вҶ’

   167вҶ’## 1.7 Glossary of Terms
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’| Term | Definition |
   170вҶ’|------|------------|
   171вҶ’| PM | Preventive Maintenance (C1, C2, C3, C4, Overhaul) |
   172вҶ’| Fixed Parameters | Resource constraints (tracks, man-hours) that bound optimization |
   173вҶ’| Run Assignment | Allocation of a specific train to a specific route for a day |
   174вҶ’| Mileage Leveling | Balancing cumulative mileage across the fleet to equalize wear |
   175вҶ’| Man-hour Leveling | Balancing workforce allocation across time periods |
   176вҶ’| Compliance Window | Acceptable date range for scheduling a PM (earliest to latest) |
   177вҶ’| Bow Wave Risk | Accumulation of deferred maintenance causing future overload |
   178вҶ’| Soft Constraint | Optimization constraint that can be violated with penalty cost |
   179вҶ’| Hard Constraint | Constraint that must never be violated (safety, physical limits) |
   180вҶ’| Strategic Horizon | Long-term planning window (1-10 years, quarterly granularity) |
   181вҶ’| Tactical Horizon | Medium-term planning window (12 weeks, daily granularity) |
   182вҶ’| Baseline | Published snapshot of a plan used as reference for drift analysis |
   183вҶ’| Drift | Deviation between actual state and baseline projection |
   184вҶ’| Scenario | Alternative version of a plan for what-if analysis |
   185вҶ’| Explanation Trace | Human-readable justification for optimizer decisions |
   186вҶ’| TCO | Total Cost of Ownership (12-year projected costs) |
   187вҶ’| Infinite Canvas | Zoomable timeline interface spanning 12 years |
   188вҶ’| Semantic Zoom | Data representation changes based on zoom level |
   189вҶ’| Bill of Materials | List of parts required for a specific PM type |
   190вҶ’| Material Reservation | Soft or hard allocation of inventory to a scheduled PM |
   191вҶ’| **Unavailability Event** | A corrective maintenance event, incident, or external factor that removes a train from service (source: Maximo CM work orders, incidents, or manual entry) |
   192вҶ’| **Service Demand** | Minimum number of trains required for passenger service by day type (weekday, Saturday, Sunday, holiday) |
   193вҶ’| **Utilization Profile** | Expected daily mileage accumulation rate based on route assignment (e.g., NSL = 350 km/day, EWL = 180 km/day) |
   194вҶ’| **Cold Start** | Scenario where PM history is incomplete during data migration, requiring estimation with confidence levels |
   195вҶ’| **Track Capability** | Specific equipment or features available on a maintenance track (e.g., pit access, crane, lifting jacks) |
   196вҶ’| **PM Capability Requirement** | Equipment or track features required to perform a specific PM type |
   197вҶ’

   216вҶ’## 1.9 Critical Dependencies
   217вҶ’
   218вҶ’The following external dependencies are **required** for MMS to produce operationally valid schedules:
   219вҶ’
   220вҶ’| Dependency | Source | Frequency | Fallback |
   221вҶ’|------------|--------|-----------|----------|
   222вҶ’| Corrective maintenance status | Maximo webhook | Real-time (or 15-min polling) | Manual status update UI with prominent warning |
   223вҶ’| Inventory positions | ERP/Maximo | Daily snapshot | Assume unlimited (with warning flag) |
   224вҶ’| Service demand | Operations input | Initial + updates | Default to 85% fleet availability |
   225вҶ’| Route utilization profiles | Operations input | Initial + updates | Fleet-wide average (with warning flag) |
   226вҶ’

    95вҶ’## 2.2 Component Responsibilities
    96вҶ’
    97вҶ’### 2.2.1 Frontend (Infinite Canvas SPA)
    98вҶ’
    99вҶ’| Responsibility | Implementation |
   100вҶ’|----------------|----------------|
   101вҶ’| Timeline rendering | deck.gl WebGL layers with semantic zoom |
   102вҶ’| Strategic view | Heatmap showing PM density per train per year |
   103вҶ’| Tactical view | Gantt blocks for 12-week PM schedule |
   104вҶ’| Operational view | Detail cards with technician, track, duration |
   105вҶ’| Baseline/drift overlay | Shadow layer showing deviation from published plan |
   106вҶ’| Constraint walls | Visual barriers at compliance window boundaries |
   107вҶ’| Drag-and-drop rescheduling | Real-time validation with constraint feedback |
   108вҶ’| Man-hour visualization | Column overlay showing utilization with/without adjustments |
   109вҶ’| Scenario comparison | Side-by-side diff view for what-if analysis |
   110вҶ’| Real-time collaboration | WebSocket sync for multi-user canvas editing |
   111вҶ’
   112вҶ’### 2.2.2 Backend API (Bun + Hono)
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’| Responsibility | Implementation |
   115вҶ’|----------------|----------------|
   116вҶ’| REST API | `/api/v1/*` endpoints with OpenAPI spec |
   117вҶ’| WebSocket hub | Real-time canvas updates and collaboration |
   118вҶ’| Authentication | JWT with refresh tokens, RBAC enforcement |
   119вҶ’| Business logic | Orchestration layer for domain services |
   120вҶ’| Task dispatch | BullMQ job queuing for async operations |
   121вҶ’| Optimizer IPC | Stream-based communication with C++ binary |
   122вҶ’| Audit logging | Immutable change records with user attribution |
   123вҶ’
   124вҶ’### 2.2.3 File Watcher Service
   125вҶ’
   126вҶ’| Responsibility | Implementation |
   127вҶ’|----------------|----------------|
   128вҶ’| Folder monitoring | chokidar watching `/data/inbox/` |
   129вҶ’| File validation | Format, size, checksum verification |
   130вҶ’| Queue dispatch | BullMQ job creation for ingestion worker |
   131вҶ’| Archive management | Move processed files to `/data/processed/` |
   132вҶ’| Quarantine handling | Move invalid files to `/data/quarantine/` |
   133вҶ’| Health alerting | Notification when files stop arriving (>2 hours) |
   134вҶ’
   135вҶ’### 2.2.4 BullMQ Workers
   136вҶ’
   137вҶ’| Worker | Responsibility | Concurrency |
   138вҶ’|--------|----------------|-------------|
   139вҶ’| `ingestion_worker` | Parse CSV, validate rows, insert to DB, handle meter swaps | 2 |
   140вҶ’| `optimizer_worker` | Spawn C++ binary, stream I/O, store results with explanations | 1 per depot |
   141вҶ’| `tactical_scheduler` | 12-week PM scheduling with material checks | 1 |
   142вҶ’| `strategic_planner` | 1-10 year capacity reservation with constraint envelope | 1 |
   143вҶ’| `manhour_leveling_worker` | Weekly/monthly workload balancing, adjustment generation | 1 |
   144вҶ’| `drift_monitor_worker` | Nightly baseline comparison, threshold alerting | 1 |
   145вҶ’| `report_worker` | Generate PDF/Excel reports | 2 |
   146вҶ’| `notification_worker` | Send email alerts and notifications | 3 |
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’### 2.2.5 C++ Optimizer Binary
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’| Responsibility | Implementation |
   151вҶ’|----------------|----------------|
   152вҶ’| Constraint solving | OR-Tools CP-SAT with soft constraint penalties |
   153вҶ’| Explanation generation | Factor-based trace for each assignment decision |
   154вҶ’| Progress streaming | NDJSON messages to stdout during solve |
   155вҶ’| Metrics emission | OpenTelemetry format to stderr |
   156вҶ’| Fallback mode | Greedy heuristic when CP-SAT times out |
   157вҶ’| Material validation | Check inventory positions for blocking parts |
   158вҶ’
   159вҶ’---
   160вҶ’
   161вҶ’## 2.3 Critical Data Flows
   162вҶ’
   163вҶ’### 2.3.1 Daily Operational Flow
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’```
   166вҶ’06:00 в”Ӯ CSV file drop (mileage.csv) вҶ’ File Watcher detects
   167вҶ’06:05 в”Ӯ Ingestion Worker: Parse, validate, insert mileage readings
   168вҶ’06:10 в”Ӯ Ingestion Worker: Update train current_mileage, detect anomalies
   169вҶ’06:15 в”Ӯ Drift Monitor: Check against baseline, flag significant deviations
   170вҶ’06:30 в”Ӯ Optimizer Worker: Generate today's run assignments
   171вҶ’      в”Ӯ   - Input: trains, routes, constraints, inventory positions
   172вҶ’      в”Ӯ   - Output: assignments with explanation traces
   173вҶ’07:00 в”Ӯ WebSocket: Push results to connected canvas clients
   174вҶ’07:00 в”Ӯ Operators: Review assignments on Infinite Canvas
   175вҶ’      в”Ӯ   - View explanation factors for each assignment
   176вҶ’      в”Ӯ   - Drag-and-drop to override if needed (with audit trail)
   177вҶ’08:00 в”Ӯ Export: Approved assignments sent to OCC system
   178вҶ’16:00 в”Ӯ Actual mileage updates (if available intra-day)
   179вҶ’22:00 в”Ӯ Optimizer Worker: Generate tomorrow's assignments
   180вҶ’```
   181вҶ’
   182вҶ’### 2.3.2 Weekly Planning Flow
   183вҶ’
   184вҶ’```
   185вҶ’Monday 06:00  в”Ӯ Strategic Planner: Run 10-year horizon update
   186вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Uses constraint envelope from tactical model
   187вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Respects published baseline (drift threshold)
   188вҶ’Monday 06:30  в”Ӯ Tactical Scheduler: Run 12-week PM scheduling
   189вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Check material availability for T+14 days (hard)
   190вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Check material availability for T+90 days (soft)
   191вҶ’Monday 07:00  в”Ӯ Man-hour Leveling: Generate 12-week projection
   192вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Identify overloaded/underutilized weeks
   193вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Propose adjustments (defer/advance/overtime)
   194вҶ’Monday 08:00  в”Ӯ WebSocket: Push updated canvas data to all clients
   195вҶ’Tuesday       в”Ӯ Planners: Review PM conflicts on canvas
   196вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Drag to resolve conflicts (audit trail)
   197вҶ’              в”Ӯ   - Review/approve man-hour adjustments
   198вҶ’Wednesday    в”Ӯ Report Worker: Generate resource utilization report
   199вҶ’Friday       в”Ӯ Notification Worker: Send weekly compliance report
   200вҶ’```
   201вҶ’
   202вҶ’### 2.3.3 Baseline Publishing Flow
   203вҶ’
   204вҶ’```
   205вҶ’User Action  в”Ӯ Planner clicks "Publish Baseline" on canvas
   206вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - System validates: no critical conflicts
   207вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - System prompts: "This will create Baseline v3.2"
   208вҶ’API Call     в”Ӯ POST /api/v1/baselines
   209вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Snapshot current pm_schedule to baseline_pm_schedule
   210вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Snapshot mileage projections to baseline_mileage_projection
   211вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Set previous baseline status to SUPERSEDED
   212вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Set new baseline status to PUBLISHED
   213вҶ’Post-Publish в”Ӯ Drift Monitor: Now compares against new baseline
   214вҶ’             в”Ӯ Canvas: Shows drift layer relative to new baseline
   215вҶ’```
   216вҶ’
   217вҶ’### 2.3.4 Scenario Comparison Flow
   218вҶ’
   219вҶ’```
   220вҶ’User Action  в”Ӯ Planner clicks "Create Scenario" on canvas
   221вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - System forks from current published baseline
   222вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - New scenario_id assigned
   223вҶ’Editing      в”Ӯ Planner makes changes within scenario
   224вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - All changes stored as deltas (event-sourced)
   225вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Original baseline data unchanged
   226вҶ’Comparison   в”Ӯ Planner clicks "Compare Scenarios"
   227вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - System materializes both scenarios
   228вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Canvas shows side-by-side diff view
   229вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Metrics: cost impact, resource utilization delta
   230вҶ’Promotion    в”Ӯ Planner clicks "Promote to Baseline"
   231вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Deltas applied to create new baseline
   232вҶ’             в”Ӯ   - Scenario marked as PROMOTED
   233вҶ’```
   234вҶ’
   235вҶ’---
   236вҶ’
   237вҶ’## 2.4 C++ Optimizer Stream Protocol
   238вҶ’
   239вҶ’### 2.4.1 Stream Architecture
   240вҶ’
   241вҶ’```
   242вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         NDJSON Stream          в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   243вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–ә в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   244вҶ’в”Ӯ   Bun Worker    в”Ӯ         stdin (input)          в”Ӯ  mms-optimizer  в”Ӯ
   245вҶ’в”Ӯ   (TypeScript)  в”Ӯ                                в”Ӯ     (C++)       в”Ӯ
   246вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   247вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ    stdout (results, NDJSON)    в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   248вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ в—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җ в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   249вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ    stderr (logs, metrics)      в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   250вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                                в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   251вҶ’```

   433вҶ’
   434вҶ’### 2.4.4 Error Handling Protocol
   435вҶ’
   436вҶ’**CRITICAL:** The C++ optimizer communicates via stdin/stdout streams. Stream failures (segfaults, hangs) must be detectable by the TypeScript worker.
   437вҶ’
   438вҶ’```typescript
   439вҶ’// Worker-side error handling (TypeScript)
   440вҶ’class OptimizerWorker {
   441вҶ’  private process: ChildProcess;
   442вҶ’  private heartbeatTimeout: NodeJS.Timeout | null = null;
   443вҶ’  private lastHeartbeat: number = 0;
   444вҶ’  
   445вҶ’  private readonly HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS = 5000;
   446вҶ’  private readonly HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS = 15000;  // 3 missed heartbeats
   447вҶ’  
   448вҶ’  async solve(request: OptimizerInput): Promise<OptimizerOutput> {
   449вҶ’    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
   450вҶ’      // Start heartbeat monitor
   451вҶ’      this.startHeartbeatMonitor(request.request_id, reject);
   452вҶ’      
   453вҶ’      // Handle stream events
   454вҶ’      this.process.stdout.on('data', (chunk) => {
   455вҶ’        for (const line of chunk.toString().split('\n')) {
   456вҶ’          if (!line.trim()) continue;
   457вҶ’          
   458вҶ’          try {
   459вҶ’            const msg = JSON.parse(line);
   460вҶ’            
   461вҶ’            switch (msg.type) {
   462вҶ’              case 'HEARTBEAT':
   463вҶ’                this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   464вҶ’                break;
   465вҶ’                
   466вҶ’              case 'PROGRESS':
   467вҶ’                this.emitProgress(msg);
   468вҶ’                break;
   469вҶ’                
   470вҶ’              case 'RESULT':
   471вҶ’                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   472вҶ’                resolve(msg);
   473вҶ’                break;
   474вҶ’                
   475вҶ’              case 'ERROR':
   476вҶ’                this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   477вҶ’                reject(new OptimizerError(msg));
   478вҶ’                break;
   479вҶ’            }
   480вҶ’          } catch (parseError) {
   481вҶ’            this.logger.warn({ line }, 'Failed to parse optimizer output');
   482вҶ’          }
   483вҶ’        }
   484вҶ’      });
   485вҶ’      
   486вҶ’      // Handle process crash
   487вҶ’      this.process.on('exit', (code, signal) => {
   488вҶ’        if (code !== 0) {
   489вҶ’          this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   490вҶ’          reject(new OptimizerError({
   491вҶ’            type: 'ERROR',
   492вҶ’            request_id: request.request_id,
   493вҶ’            error_code: signal === 'SIGSEGV' ? 'SEGFAULT' : 'INTERNAL_ERROR',
   494вҶ’            error_message: `Process exited with code ${code}, signal ${signal}`,
   495вҶ’            recovery_suggestion: 'Check optimizer logs and input data'
   496вҶ’          }));
   497вҶ’        }
   498вҶ’      });
   499вҶ’      
   500вҶ’      // Send request
   501вҶ’      this.process.stdin.write(JSON.stringify(request) + '\n');
   502вҶ’    });
   503вҶ’  }
   504вҶ’  
   505вҶ’  private startHeartbeatMonitor(requestId: string, reject: Function): void {
   506вҶ’    this.lastHeartbeat = Date.now();
   507вҶ’    
   508вҶ’    this.heartbeatTimeout = setInterval(() => {
   509вҶ’      const elapsed = Date.now() - this.lastHeartbeat;
   510вҶ’      
   511вҶ’      if (elapsed > this.HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT_MS) {
   512вҶ’        this.stopHeartbeatMonitor();
   513вҶ’        
   514вҶ’        // Kill hung process
   515вҶ’        this.process.kill('SIGKILL');
   516вҶ’        
   517вҶ’        reject(new OptimizerError({
   518вҶ’          type: 'ERROR',
   519вҶ’          request_id: requestId,
   520вҶ’          error_code: 'TIMEOUT',
   521вҶ’          error_message: `No heartbeat received for ${elapsed}ms`,
   522вҶ’          recovery_suggestion: 'Optimizer may be stuck. Consider reducing problem size.'
   523вҶ’        }));
   524вҶ’      }
   525вҶ’    }, this.HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL_MS);
   526вҶ’  }
   527вҶ’  
   528вҶ’  private stopHeartbeatMonitor(): void {
   529вҶ’    if (this.heartbeatTimeout) {
   530вҶ’      clearInterval(this.heartbeatTimeout);
   531вҶ’      this.heartbeatTimeout = null;
   532вҶ’    }
   533вҶ’  }
   534вҶ’}
   535вҶ’
   536вҶ’class OptimizerError extends Error {
   537вҶ’  readonly errorCode: string;
   538вҶ’  readonly requestId: string;
   539вҶ’  readonly recoverySuggestion: string;
   540вҶ’  readonly stackTrace?: string;
   541вҶ’  
   542вҶ’  constructor(msg: ErrorMessage) {
   543вҶ’    super(msg.error_message);
   544вҶ’    this.name = 'OptimizerError';
   545вҶ’    this.errorCode = msg.error_code;
   546вҶ’    this.requestId = msg.request_id;
   547вҶ’    this.recoverySuggestion = msg.recovery_suggestion;
   548вҶ’    this.stackTrace = msg.stack_trace;
   549вҶ’  }
   550вҶ’}
   551вҶ’```
   552вҶ’
   553вҶ’**C++ Implementation Notes:**
   554вҶ’
   555вҶ’The C++ binary must:
   556вҶ’1. Install signal handlers for SIGSEGV, SIGABRT, SIGFPE
   557вҶ’2. Send heartbeat messages every 5 seconds during solve
   558вҶ’3. Catch exceptions and emit ERROR messages before exit
   559вҶ’4. Flush stdout after every message (line-buffered)
   560вҶ’
   561вҶ’```cpp
   562вҶ’// Signal handler example (C++)
   563вҶ’void signalHandler(int signal) {
   564вҶ’    nlohmann::json error;
   565вҶ’    error["type"] = "ERROR";
   566вҶ’    error["request_id"] = currentRequestId;
   567вҶ’    error["error_code"] = (signal == SIGSEGV) ? "SEGFAULT" : "INTERNAL_ERROR";
   568вҶ’    error["error_message"] = strsignal(signal);
   569вҶ’    error["stack_trace"] = captureStackTrace();
   570вҶ’    error["recovery_suggestion"] = "Internal optimizer error. Check input data.";
   571вҶ’    
   572вҶ’    std::cout << error.dump() << std::endl;
   573вҶ’    std::cout.flush();
   574вҶ’    
   575вҶ’    _exit(128 + signal);
   576вҶ’}
   577вҶ’```
   578вҶ’
   579вҶ’The Constraint Aggregation Layer ensures consistency between Strategic and Tactical planning by computing capacity envelopes.
   580вҶ’
   581вҶ’### 2.5.1 Purpose
   582вҶ’
   583вҶ’The Strategic Planner operates at quarterly granularity and cannot evaluate detailed daily constraints. However, it must not create plans that the Tactical Scheduler will reject. The Constraint Aggregation Layer bridges this gap.
   584вҶ’
   585вҶ’### 2.5.2 Aggregation Process
   586вҶ’
   587вҶ’```typescript
   588вҶ’interface ConstraintEnvelope {
   589вҶ’  period: {
   590вҶ’    start: Date;  // Quarter start
   591вҶ’    end: Date;    // Quarter end
   592вҶ’  };
   593вҶ’  
   594вҶ’  // Track capacity (aggregated from daily track availability)
   595вҶ’  track_capacity: {
   596вҶ’    depot_id: string;
   597вҶ’    track_type: 'LIGHT' | 'HEAVY' | 'OVERHAUL';
   598вҶ’    total_slot_days: number;      // Sum of available days in quarter
   599вҶ’    reserved_slot_days: number;   // Already committed
   600вҶ’    available_slot_days: number;  // Remaining capacity
   601вҶ’  }[];
   602вҶ’  
   603вҶ’  // Labor capacity (aggregated from weekly man-hour projections)
   604вҶ’  labor_capacity: {
   605вҶ’    depot_id: string;
   606вҶ’    regular_hours: number;        // Total regular hours in quarter
   607вҶ’    overtime_hours: number;       // Total overtime available
   608вҶ’    committed_hours: number;      // Already scheduled
   609вҶ’    available_hours: number;      // Remaining capacity
   610вҶ’  }[];
   611вҶ’  
   612вҶ’  // Material capacity (aggregated from inventory projections)
   613вҶ’  material_capacity: {
   614вҶ’    depot_id: string;
   615вҶ’    part_category: string;        // e.g., 'BOGIE_KIT', 'BRAKE_SET'
   616вҶ’    expected_available: number;   // Projected inventory
   617вҶ’    committed: number;            // Reserved for scheduled PMs
   618вҶ’    available: number;            // Remaining for new scheduling
   619вҶ’  }[];
   620вҶ’  
   621вҶ’  // Constraints from tactical model
   622вҶ’  blocked_trains: {
   623вҶ’    train_id: string;
   624вҶ’    blocked_weeks: number;        // Weeks unavailable in quarter
   625вҶ’    reason: string;
   626вҶ’  }[];
   627вҶ’}
   628вҶ’
   629вҶ’class ConstraintAggregationService {
   630вҶ’  /**
   631вҶ’   * Generate constraint envelope for strategic planning
   632вҶ’   */
   633вҶ’  async generateEnvelope(
   634вҶ’    quarterStart: Date,
   635вҶ’    quarterEnd: Date,
   636вҶ’    depotIds: string[]
   637вҶ’  ): Promise<ConstraintEnvelope> {
   638вҶ’    // 1. Aggregate track availability
   639вҶ’    const trackCapacity = await this.aggregateTrackCapacity(
   640вҶ’      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   641вҶ’    );
   642вҶ’    
   643вҶ’    // 2. Aggregate labor capacity
   644вҶ’    const laborCapacity = await this.aggregateLaborCapacity(
   645вҶ’      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   646вҶ’    );
   647вҶ’    
   648вҶ’    // 3. Aggregate material projections
   649вҶ’    const materialCapacity = await this.aggregateMaterialCapacity(
   650вҶ’      quarterStart, quarterEnd, depotIds
   651вҶ’    );
   652вҶ’    
   653вҶ’    // 4. Identify blocked trains from tactical constraints
   654вҶ’    const blockedTrains = await this.identifyBlockedTrains(
   655вҶ’      quarterStart, quarterEnd
   656вҶ’    );
   657вҶ’    
   658вҶ’    return {
   659вҶ’      period: { start: quarterStart, end: quarterEnd },
   660вҶ’      track_capacity: trackCapacity,
   661вҶ’      labor_capacity: laborCapacity,
   662вҶ’      material_capacity: materialCapacity,
   663вҶ’      blocked_trains: blockedTrains
   664вҶ’    };
   665вҶ’  }
   666вҶ’  
   667вҶ’  /**
   668вҶ’   * Validate strategic allocation against constraint envelope
   669вҶ’   */
   670вҶ’  async validateStrategicAllocation(
   671вҶ’    allocation: StrategicAllocation,
   672вҶ’    envelope: ConstraintEnvelope
   673вҶ’  ): Promise<ValidationResult> {
   674вҶ’    const violations: ConstraintViolation[] = [];
   675вҶ’    
   676вҶ’    // Check track capacity
   677вҶ’    for (const trackReq of allocation.track_requirements) {
   678вҶ’      const available = envelope.track_capacity.find(
   679вҶ’        t => t.depot_id === trackReq.depot_id && 
   680вҶ’             t.track_type === trackReq.track_type
   681вҶ’      );
   682вҶ’      
   683вҶ’      if (available && trackReq.slot_days > available.available_slot_days) {
   684вҶ’        violations.push({
   685вҶ’          type: 'TRACK_OVERCAPACITY',
   686вҶ’          severity: 'HARD',
   687вҶ’          message: `${trackReq.track_type} track at ${trackReq.depot_id} overbooked by ${trackReq.slot_days - available.available_slot_days} days`
   688вҶ’        });
   689вҶ’      }
   690вҶ’    }
   691вҶ’    
   692вҶ’    // Check labor capacity
   693вҶ’    // Check material capacity
   694вҶ’    // ... similar validation logic
   695вҶ’    
   696вҶ’    return {
   697вҶ’      valid: violations.filter(v => v.severity === 'HARD').length === 0,
   698вҶ’      violations
   699вҶ’    };
   700вҶ’  }
   701вҶ’}
   702вҶ’```
   703вҶ’
   704вҶ’---
   705вҶ’
   706вҶ’## 2.6 Key Design Principles
   707вҶ’
   708вҶ’### 2.6.1 Explainability First
   709вҶ’
   710вҶ’Every optimization decision must be traceable to specific constraints:
   711вҶ’
   712вҶ’- **Run assignments**: Include explanation factors in API response
   713вҶ’- **PM scheduling**: Log which constraints drove date selection
   714вҶ’- **Man-hour adjustments**: Record rationale for each proposed change
   715вҶ’- **Strategic allocations**: Document constraint envelope used
   716вҶ’
   717вҶ’### 2.6.2 Graceful Degradation
   718вҶ’
   719вҶ’The system must provide useful output even when optimal solutions are impossible:
   720вҶ’
   721вҶ’- **C++ solver timeout**: Fall back to TypeScript greedy solver
   722вҶ’- **Material unavailability**: Flag but continue with labor optimization
   723вҶ’- **Constraint conflicts**: Return least-bad solution with violation list
   724вҶ’- **Data quality issues**: Proceed with validated subset, quarantine bad records
   725вҶ’
   726вҶ’### 2.6.3 Audit Everything
   727вҶ’
   728вҶ’For 12-year government contract compliance:
   729вҶ’
   730вҶ’- **All schedule changes**: Who, when, what, why
   731вҶ’- **All baseline publications**: Snapshot contents, approval chain
   732вҶ’- **All scenario promotions**: Delta applied, approval
   733вҶ’- **All system decisions**: Optimizer inputs/outputs preserved
   734вҶ’
   735вҶ’### 2.6.4 Stable Long-Term Planning
   736вҶ’
   737вҶ’Prevent "jittery" forecasts that erode planner trust:
   738вҶ’
   739вҶ’- **Published baselines**: Immutable reference points
   740вҶ’- **Drift thresholds**: Only re-plan when variance exceeds 5%
   741вҶ’- **Dampening**: Strategic forecasts use rolling averages, not daily deltas
   742вҶ’- **Explicit triggers**: Users control when strategic re-planning occurs
   743вҶ’

    63вҶ’## 7.3 Mileage Data Ingestion
    64вҶ’
    65вҶ’### 7.3.1 CSV File Format
    66вҶ’
    67вҶ’**Filename Pattern:** `mileage_YYYYMMDD.csv`  
    68вҶ’**Encoding:** UTF-8  
    69вҶ’**Delimiter:** Comma  
    70вҶ’**Header:** Required
    71вҶ’
    72вҶ’```csv
    73вҶ’train_number,reading_date,odometer_km,source
    74вҶ’1001,2026-01-14,245152,ATS
    75вҶ’1002,2026-01-14,248523,ATS
    76вҶ’1003,2026-01-14,242087,ATS
    77вҶ’```
    78вҶ’
    79вҶ’### 7.3.2 Field Specifications
    80вҶ’
    81вҶ’| Field | Type | Required | Validation |
    82вҶ’|-------|------|----------|------------|
    83вҶ’| `train_number` | string | Yes | Must exist in trains table |
    84вҶ’| `reading_date` | date | Yes | Format: YYYY-MM-DD, not future |
    85вҶ’| `odometer_km` | integer | Yes | Positive, вүӨ previous + 2000 |
    86вҶ’| `source` | string | No | Default: 'CSV' |
    87вҶ’
    88вҶ’### 7.3.3 Validation Rules
    89вҶ’
    90вҶ’```typescript
    91вҶ’interface MileageValidationRules {
    92вҶ’  // Format validation
    93вҶ’  maxFileSize: '10MB';
    94вҶ’  requiredColumns: ['train_number', 'reading_date', 'odometer_km'];
    95вҶ’  dateFormat: 'YYYY-MM-DD';
    96вҶ’  
    97вҶ’  // Business validation
    98вҶ’  maxDailyIncrease: 2000;           // km - anything more is anomaly
    99вҶ’  maxDailyDecrease: 0;              // Negative not allowed without meter event
   100вҶ’  duplicateHandling: 'UPDATE';       // UPDATE or REJECT
   101вҶ’  unknownTrainHandling: 'QUARANTINE'; // QUARANTINE or REJECT_FILE
   102вҶ’  
   103вҶ’  // Timing
   104вҶ’  expectedArrivalWindow: {
   105вҶ’    start: '05:00',                 // Local time
   106вҶ’    end: '07:00'
   107вҶ’  };
   108вҶ’  alertIfMissingAfter: '08:00';
   109вҶ’}
   110вҶ’```
   111вҶ’
   112вҶ’### 7.3.4 Error Handling
   113вҶ’
   114вҶ’```typescript
   115вҶ’interface IngestionError {
   116вҶ’  severity: 'WARNING' | 'ERROR' | 'CRITICAL';
   117вҶ’  code: string;
   118вҶ’  message: string;
   119вҶ’  row?: number;
   120вҶ’  field?: string;
   121вҶ’  value?: any;
   122вҶ’  action: 'SKIP_ROW' | 'QUARANTINE_FILE' | 'ALERT_ADMIN';
   123вҶ’}
   124вҶ’
   125вҶ’const ERROR_CODES = {
   126вҶ’  // File-level errors
   127вҶ’  'FILE_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'File size exceeds limit' },
   128вҶ’  'FILE_002': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Invalid file format' },
   129вҶ’  'FILE_003': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Missing required columns' },
   130вҶ’  
   131вҶ’  // Row-level errors
   132вҶ’  'ROW_001': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Unknown train number' },
   133вҶ’  'ROW_002': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Future date not allowed' },
   134вҶ’  'ROW_003': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Negative daily mileage' },
   135вҶ’  'ROW_004': { severity: 'WARNING', message: 'Spike detected (>2000km/day)' },
   136вҶ’  'ROW_005': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Invalid odometer value' },
   137вҶ’  
   138вҶ’  // System errors
   139вҶ’  'SYS_001': { severity: 'CRITICAL', message: 'Database connection failed' },
   140вҶ’  'SYS_002': { severity: 'ERROR', message: 'Transaction rollback' }
   141вҶ’};
   142вҶ’```
   143вҶ’

   146вҶ’## 7.4 Maximo Integration
   147вҶ’
   148вҶ’### 7.4.1 Integration Architecture
   149вҶ’
   150вҶ’```
   151вҶ’в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ         в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   152вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   153вҶ’в”Ӯ      MMS        в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ   Integration   в”Ӯв—„в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв–әв”Ӯ     Maximo      в”Ӯ
   154вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ     Service     в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   155вҶ’в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ         в”Ӯ                 в”Ӯ
   156вҶ’в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ         в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   157вҶ’        в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
   158вҶ’        в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
   159вҶ’   в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ                в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
   160вҶ’   в”Ӯ Shadow  в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ Transformв”Ӯ               в”Ӯ  REST   в”Ӯ
   161вҶ’   в”Ӯ Records в”Ӯ                в”Ӯ & Map    в”Ӯ               в”Ӯ  API    в”Ӯ
   162вҶ’   в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ                в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ               в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ
   163вҶ’```
   164вҶ’
   165вҶ’### 7.4.2 Shadow Record Pattern
   166вҶ’
   167вҶ’MMS maintains "shadow records" that mirror Maximo work orders, allowing operations to continue if Maximo is unavailable.
   168вҶ’
   169вҶ’```typescript
   170вҶ’interface MaximoShadowRecord {
   171вҶ’  mmsId: string;                    // MMS primary key
   172вҶ’  maximoWoId: string | null;        // Maximo work order ID (null if not synced)
   173вҶ’  syncStatus: 'PENDING' | 'SYNCED' | 'CONFLICT' | 'ERROR';
   174вҶ’  lastSyncAt: Date | null;
   175вҶ’  lastSyncError: string | null;
   176вҶ’  
   177вҶ’  // MMS data (source of truth for scheduling)
   178вҶ’  mmsData: {
   179вҶ’    trainId: string;
   180вҶ’    pmTypeCode: string;
   181вҶ’    targetDate: Date;
   182вҶ’    status: string;
   183вҶ’  };
   184вҶ’  
   185вҶ’  // Maximo data (for reconciliation)
   186вҶ’  maximoData: {
   187вҶ’    status: string;
   188вҶ’    actualStart: Date | null;
   189вҶ’    actualFinish: Date | null;
   190вҶ’    actualLabor: number | null;
   191вҶ’  } | null;
   192вҶ’}
   193вҶ’```
   194вҶ’
   195вҶ’### 7.4.3 Maximo REST API Endpoints
   196вҶ’
   197вҶ’**Base URL:** `https://maximo.metro.example/maximo/oslc`
   198вҶ’
   199вҶ’| Operation | Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
   200вҶ’|-----------|--------|----------|---------|
   201вҶ’| Get Work Order | GET | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Fetch WO details |
   202вҶ’| Create Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo` | Create new WO |
   203вҶ’| Update Work Order | POST | `/os/mxwo/{wonum}` | Update WO status |
   204вҶ’| List Work Orders | GET | `/os/mxwo` | Query WOs by filter |
   205вҶ’| Get Asset | GET | `/os/mxasset/{assetnum}` | Fetch asset (train) |
   206вҶ’| List Inventory | GET | `/os/mxinventory` | Query inventory |
   207вҶ’
   208вҶ’### 7.4.4 Work Order Creation
   209вҶ’
   210вҶ’When MMS schedules a PM, it creates a shadow record. On sync:
   211вҶ’
   212вҶ’```typescript
   213вҶ’interface MaximoWorkOrderCreate {
   214вҶ’  // Required fields
   215вҶ’  siteid: string;                  // 'METRO'
   216вҶ’  woclass: string;                 // 'WORKORDER'
   217вҶ’  worktype: string;                // 'PM'
   218вҶ’  assetnum: string;                // Train number
   219вҶ’  description: string;             // PM description
   220вҶ’  schedstart: string;              // ISO 8601 date
   221вҶ’  schedfinish: string;             // ISO 8601 date
   222вҶ’  
   223вҶ’  // Optional fields
   224вҶ’  location: string;                // Depot code
   225вҶ’  estlabhrs: number;               // Estimated hours
   226вҶ’  pmnum: string;                   // PM type reference
   227вҶ’  
   228вҶ’  // Custom fields (metro-specific)
   229вҶ’  mms_schedule_id: string;         // Link back to MMS
   230вҶ’  mms_constraint_start: string;    // Compliance window start
   231вҶ’  mms_constraint_end: string;      // Compliance window end
   232вҶ’}
   233вҶ’
   234вҶ’// Example
   235вҶ’const createWorkOrder = async (pm: PMSchedule): Promise<string> => {
   236вҶ’  const payload: MaximoWorkOrderCreate = {
   237вҶ’    siteid: 'METRO',
   238вҶ’    woclass: 'WORKORDER',
   239вҶ’    worktype: 'PM',
   240вҶ’    assetnum: pm.train.trainNumber,
   241вҶ’    description: `${pm.pmType.pmCode} - ${pm.pmType.pmName}`,
   242вҶ’    schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   243вҶ’    schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
   244вҶ’    location: pm.assignedDepot.code,
   245вҶ’    estlabhrs: pm.assignedHours,
   246вҶ’    pmnum: pm.pmType.pmCode,
   247вҶ’    mms_schedule_id: pm.id,
   248вҶ’    mms_constraint_start: pm.constraintStart.toISOString(),
   249вҶ’    mms_constraint_end: pm.constraintEnd.toISOString()
   250вҶ’  };
   251вҶ’  
   252вҶ’  const response = await maximoClient.post('/os/mxwo', payload);
   253вҶ’  return response.data.wonum;
   254вҶ’};
   255вҶ’```
   256вҶ’
   257вҶ’### 7.4.5 Work Order Status Mapping
   258вҶ’
   259вҶ’| Maximo Status | MMS Status | Sync Action | MMS Edit Lock |
   260вҶ’|---------------|------------|-------------|---------------|
   261вҶ’| WAPPR | SCHEDULED | None (initial state) | вқҢ Unlocked |
   262вҶ’| APPR | SCHEDULED | None | вқҢ Unlocked |
   263вҶ’| INPRG | IN_PROGRESS | Update MMS status | вң… **LOCKED** |
   264вҶ’| COMP | COMPLETED | Update MMS, record actuals | вң… **LOCKED** |
   265вҶ’| CLOSE | COMPLETED | Final state | вң… **LOCKED** |
   266вҶ’| CAN | CANCELLED | Update MMS status | вң… **LOCKED** |
   267вҶ’
   268вҶ’### 7.4.6 Status-Based Edit Locking
   269вҶ’
   270вҶ’**CRITICAL:** When a Maximo work order is in progress (INPRG), the corresponding PM record in MMS must be **read-only**. This prevents race conditions where a planner reschedules a PM that technicians are actively working on.
   271вҶ’
   272вҶ’```typescript
   273вҶ’/**
   274вҶ’ * Validate PM edit request against Maximo status.
   275вҶ’ * MUST be called before any PM schedule modification.
   276вҶ’ * 
   277вҶ’ * This addresses the race condition where:
   278вҶ’ * 1. Technician opens work order on tablet (Maximo status = INPRG)
   279вҶ’ * 2. Planner reschedules PM in MMS (unaware of in-progress work)
   280вҶ’ * 3. Maximo sync fails or overwrites technician's work
   281вҶ’ * 
   282вҶ’ * Operational reality MUST override planning data.
   283вҶ’ */
   284вҶ’class PMEditValidator {
   285вҶ’  async validateEdit(pmId: string): Promise<EditValidationResult> {
   286вҶ’    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   287вҶ’    
   288вҶ’    // Check 1: Maximo status lock
   289вҶ’    if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status) {
   290вҶ’      const lockedStatuses = ['INPRG', 'COMP', 'CLOSE', 'CAN'];
   291вҶ’      
   292вҶ’      if (lockedStatuses.includes(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)) {
   293вҶ’        return {
   294вҶ’          valid: false,
   295вҶ’          reason: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK',
   296вҶ’          message: `Cannot modify: Work order is ${this.formatStatus(pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status)} in Maximo`,
   297вҶ’          maximoStatus: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status,
   298вҶ’          maximoWonum: pm.maximoWoId,
   299вҶ’          suggestion: pm.shadowRecord.maximoData.status === 'INPRG' 
   300вҶ’            ? 'Wait for technicians to complete work, or contact supervisor to cancel in Maximo first'
   301вҶ’            : 'This PM has been completed/cancelled. Create a new PM if rescheduling is needed.'
   302вҶ’        };
   303вҶ’      }
   304вҶ’    }
   305вҶ’    
   306вҶ’    // Check 2: Pending sync conflict
   307вҶ’    if (pm.shadowRecord?.syncStatus === 'CONFLICT') {
   308вҶ’      return {
   309вҶ’        valid: false,
   310вҶ’        reason: 'SYNC_CONFLICT',
   311вҶ’        message: 'Unresolved sync conflict exists. Resolve before editing.',
   312вҶ’        suggestion: 'Go to Integration > Conflicts to resolve'
   313вҶ’      };
   314вҶ’    }
   315вҶ’    
   316вҶ’    // Check 3: Baseline lock (optional, if locked in current baseline)
   317вҶ’    if (pm.lockedInBaseline) {
   318вҶ’      return {
   319вҶ’        valid: false,
   320вҶ’        reason: 'BASELINE_LOCK',
   321вҶ’        message: 'PM is locked in published baseline',
   322вҶ’        suggestion: 'Unlock in baseline editor or create scenario to test changes'
   323вҶ’      };
   324вҶ’    }
   325вҶ’    
   326вҶ’    return { valid: true };
   327вҶ’  }
   328вҶ’  
   329вҶ’  private formatStatus(status: string): string {
   330вҶ’    const statusLabels: Record<string, string> = {
   331вҶ’      'INPRG': 'In Progress',
   332вҶ’      'COMP': 'Completed',
   333вҶ’      'CLOSE': 'Closed',
   334вҶ’      'CAN': 'Cancelled'
   335вҶ’    };
   336вҶ’    return statusLabels[status] || status;
   337вҶ’  }
   338вҶ’}
   339вҶ’
   340вҶ’interface EditValidationResult {
   341вҶ’  valid: boolean;
   342вҶ’  reason?: 'MAXIMO_STATUS_LOCK' | 'SYNC_CONFLICT' | 'BASELINE_LOCK';
   343вҶ’  message?: string;
   344вҶ’  maximoStatus?: string;
   345вҶ’  maximoWonum?: string;
   346вҶ’  suggestion?: string;
   347вҶ’}
   348вҶ’```
   349вҶ’
   350вҶ’**API Integration:**
   351вҶ’
   352вҶ’All PM modification endpoints MUST call this validator:
   353вҶ’
   354вҶ’```typescript
   355вҶ’// In PM Schedule API (Part 5)
   356вҶ’app.patch('/api/v1/pm-schedule/:id', async (req, res) => {
   357вҶ’  const validation = await pmEditValidator.validateEdit(req.params.id);
   358вҶ’  
   359вҶ’  if (!validation.valid) {
   360вҶ’    return res.status(423).json({  // 423 Locked
   361вҶ’      error: validation.reason,
   362вҶ’      message: validation.message,
   363вҶ’      suggestion: validation.suggestion,
   364вҶ’      maximoStatus: validation.maximoStatus
   365вҶ’    });
   366вҶ’  }
   367вҶ’  
   368вҶ’  // Proceed with update...
   369вҶ’});
   370вҶ’```
   371вҶ’
   372вҶ’**Canvas UI Integration:**
   373вҶ’
   374вҶ’When displaying PM blocks, check lock status and show visual indicator:
   375вҶ’
   376вҶ’```typescript
   377вҶ’// In Canvas layer (Part 6)
   378вҶ’function getPMBlockStyle(pm: PMSchedule): PMBlockStyle {
   379вҶ’  if (pm.shadowRecord?.maximoData?.status === 'INPRG') {
   380вҶ’    return {
   381вҶ’      cursor: 'not-allowed',
   382вҶ’      opacity: 0.7,
   383вҶ’      border: '2px dashed #F59E0B',  // Amber dashed = in progress
   384вҶ’      draggable: false,
   385вҶ’      tooltip: 'In Progress in Maximo - cannot reschedule'
   386вҶ’    };
   387вҶ’  }
   388вҶ’  // ... normal styling
   389вҶ’}
   390вҶ’```
   391вҶ’
   392вҶ’### 7.4.7 Bidirectional Sync Logic
   393вҶ’
   394вҶ’```typescript
   395вҶ’class MaximoSyncService {
   396вҶ’  /**
   397вҶ’   * Push MMS changes to Maximo
   398вҶ’   */
   399вҶ’  async pushToMaximo(pmId: string): Promise<SyncResult> {
   400вҶ’    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   401вҶ’    
   402вҶ’    if (!pm.maximoWoId) {
   403вҶ’      // Create new work order
   404вҶ’      const wonum = await this.createWorkOrder(pm);
   405вҶ’      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   406вҶ’        maximoWoId: wonum, 
   407вҶ’        syncStatus: 'SYNCED' 
   408вҶ’      });
   409вҶ’      return { action: 'CREATED', wonum };
   410вҶ’    }
   411вҶ’    
   412вҶ’    // Update existing work order
   413вҶ’    const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
   414вҶ’    
   415вҶ’    if (maximoWO.status === 'INPRG' || maximoWO.status === 'COMP') {
   416вҶ’      // Cannot update in-progress or completed WOs
   417вҶ’      return { action: 'SKIPPED', reason: 'WO already in progress' };
   418вҶ’    }
   419вҶ’    
   420вҶ’    // Check for conflicts
   421вҶ’    if (new Date(maximoWO.changedate) > pm.shadowRecord.lastSyncAt) {
   422вҶ’      await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { syncStatus: 'CONFLICT' });
   423вҶ’      return { action: 'CONFLICT', maximoData: maximoWO };
   424вҶ’    }
   425вҶ’    
   426вҶ’    // Push update
   427вҶ’    await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
   428вҶ’      schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   429вҶ’      schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString(),
   430вҶ’      location: pm.assignedDepot.code
   431вҶ’    });
   432вҶ’    
   433вҶ’    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   434вҶ’      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
   435вҶ’      lastSyncAt: new Date()
   436вҶ’    });
   437вҶ’    
   438вҶ’    return { action: 'UPDATED', wonum: pm.maximoWoId };
   439вҶ’  }
   440вҶ’  
   441вҶ’  /**
   442вҶ’   * Pull Maximo changes to MMS
   443вҶ’   */
   444вҶ’  async pullFromMaximo(): Promise<PullResult> {
   445вҶ’    // Get recently modified work orders
   446вҶ’    const modifiedSince = await this.getLastPullTimestamp();
   447вҶ’    const workOrders = await this.queryMaximoWorkOrders({
   448вҶ’      worktype: 'PM',
   449вҶ’      changedate: `>${modifiedSince.toISOString()}`
   450вҶ’    });
   451вҶ’    
   452вҶ’    const results: PullResult = {
   453вҶ’      processed: 0,
   454вҶ’      updated: 0,
   455вҶ’      conflicts: 0,
   456вҶ’      errors: 0
   457вҶ’    };
   458вҶ’    
   459вҶ’    for (const wo of workOrders) {
   460вҶ’      results.processed++;
   461вҶ’      
   462вҶ’      try {
   463вҶ’        const pm = await this.findPMByMaximoId(wo.wonum);
   464вҶ’        
   465вҶ’        if (!pm) {
   466вҶ’          // Work order not managed by MMS
   467вҶ’          continue;
   468вҶ’        }
   469вҶ’        
   470вҶ’        // Status change
   471вҶ’        if (wo.status !== pm.shadowRecord.maximoData?.status) {
   472вҶ’          await this.handleStatusChange(pm, wo);
   473вҶ’          results.updated++;
   474вҶ’        }
   475вҶ’        
   476вҶ’        // Actuals recorded
   477вҶ’        if (wo.actlabhrs && !pm.actualHours) {
   478вҶ’          await this.recordActuals(pm, wo);
   479вҶ’          results.updated++;
   480вҶ’        }
   481вҶ’        
   482вҶ’      } catch (error) {
   483вҶ’        results.errors++;
   484вҶ’        this.logger.error({ wo, error }, 'Pull processing failed');
   485вҶ’      }
   486вҶ’    }
   487вҶ’    
   488вҶ’    await this.setLastPullTimestamp(new Date());
   489вҶ’    return results;
   490вҶ’  }
   491вҶ’  
   492вҶ’  /**
   493вҶ’   * Resolve sync conflict
   494вҶ’   */
   495вҶ’  async resolveConflict(
   496вҶ’    pmId: string, 
   497вҶ’    resolution: 'USE_MMS' | 'USE_MAXIMO'
   498вҶ’  ): Promise<void> {
   499вҶ’    const pm = await this.getPMWithShadow(pmId);
   500вҶ’    
   501вҶ’    if (resolution === 'USE_MMS') {
   502вҶ’      // Force push MMS data to Maximo
   503вҶ’      await this.updateWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId, {
   504вҶ’        schedstart: pm.targetDate.toISOString(),
   505вҶ’        schedfinish: addDays(pm.targetDate, pm.pmType.minTrackDays).toISOString()
   506вҶ’      }, { force: true });
   507вҶ’    } else {
   508вҶ’      // Apply Maximo data to MMS
   509вҶ’      const maximoWO = await this.getMaximoWorkOrder(pm.maximoWoId);
   510вҶ’      await this.updatePMFromMaximo(pm.id, maximoWO);
   511вҶ’    }
   512вҶ’    
   513вҶ’    await this.updateShadowRecord(pm.id, { 
   514вҶ’      syncStatus: 'SYNCED',
   515вҶ’      lastSyncAt: new Date()
   516вҶ’    });
   517вҶ’  }
   518вҶ’}
   519вҶ’```
   520вҶ’

   524вҶ’interface InventorySnapshotConfig {

   594вҶ’## 7.6 Email Notification System
   595вҶ’
   596вҶ’### 7.6.1 SMTP Configuration
   597вҶ’
   598вҶ’```typescript
   599вҶ’interface SMTPConfig {
   600вҶ’  host: string;
   601вҶ’  port: number;
   602вҶ’  secure: boolean;
   603вҶ’  auth: {
   604вҶ’    user: string;
   605вҶ’    pass: string;  // From secrets manager
   606вҶ’  };
   607вҶ’  from: {
   608вҶ’    name: string;  // 'MMS Notifications'
   609вҶ’    address: string;  // 'mms-noreply@metro.example'
   610вҶ’  };
   611вҶ’}
   612вҶ’```
   613вҶ’
   614вҶ’### 7.6.2 Notification Types
   615вҶ’
   616вҶ’| Type | Trigger | Recipients | Template |
   617вҶ’|------|---------|------------|----------|
   618вҶ’| `DRIFT_ALERT` | Drift threshold exceeded | Planners | drift_alert.html |
   619вҶ’| `OVERLOAD_WARNING` | Week utilization >100% | Depot managers | overload_warning.html |
   620вҶ’| `PM_REMINDER` | PM due in 7 days | Depot managers | pm_reminder.html |
   621вҶ’| `BASELINE_PUBLISHED` | New baseline published | All planners | baseline_published.html |

   706вҶ’## 7.7 Report Generation
   707вҶ’
   708вҶ’### 7.7.1 Report Types
   709вҶ’
   710вҶ’| Report | Format | Schedule | Description |

   713вҶ’| Monthly TCO | Excel | 1st of month | Cost breakdown by category |
   714вҶ’| Fleet Mileage | Excel | Daily 08:00 | Mileage readings and anomalies |

   718вҶ’### 7.7.2 Report Generation Service
   719вҶ’
   720вҶ’```typescript
   721вҶ’class ReportGenerationService {
   722вҶ’  async generateReport(
   723вҶ’    type: ReportType,
   724вҶ’    params: ReportParams
   725вҶ’  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   726вҶ’    const template = await this.loadTemplate(type);
   727вҶ’    const data = await this.fetchReportData(type, params);
   728вҶ’    
   729вҶ’    switch (template.format) {
   730вҶ’      case 'PDF':
   731вҶ’        return this.generatePDF(template, data);
   732вҶ’      case 'XLSX':
   733вҶ’        return this.generateExcel(template, data);
   734вҶ’      default:
   735вҶ’        throw new Error(`Unsupported format: ${template.format}`);
   736вҶ’    }
   737вҶ’  }
   738вҶ’  
   739вҶ’  private async generatePDF(
   740вҶ’    template: ReportTemplate,
   741вҶ’    data: any
   742вҶ’  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   743вҶ’    // Use Puppeteer for PDF generation
   744вҶ’    const html = await this.renderTemplate(template.htmlTemplate, data);
   745вҶ’    
   746вҶ’    const browser = await puppeteer.launch({ headless: true });
   747вҶ’    const page = await browser.newPage();
   748вҶ’    await page.setContent(html);
   749вҶ’    
   750вҶ’    const pdfBuffer = await page.pdf({
   751вҶ’      format: 'A4',
   752вҶ’      margin: { top: '1cm', right: '1cm', bottom: '1cm', left: '1cm' },
   753вҶ’      printBackground: true
   754вҶ’    });
   755вҶ’    
   756вҶ’    await browser.close();
   757вҶ’    
   758вҶ’    // Store in MinIO
   759вҶ’    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.pdf`;
   760вҶ’    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, pdfBuffer);
   761вҶ’    
   762вҶ’    return {
   763вҶ’      filename,
   764вҶ’      url,
   765вҶ’      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
   766вҶ’      sizeBytes: pdfBuffer.length
   767вҶ’    };
   768вҶ’  }
   769вҶ’  
   770вҶ’  private async generateExcel(
   771вҶ’    template: ReportTemplate,
   772вҶ’    data: any
   773вҶ’  ): Promise<GeneratedReport> {
   774вҶ’    // Use ExcelJS for Excel generation
   775вҶ’    const workbook = new ExcelJS.Workbook();
   776вҶ’    
   777вҶ’    for (const sheetDef of template.sheets) {
   778вҶ’      const sheet = workbook.addWorksheet(sheetDef.name);
   779вҶ’      
   780вҶ’      // Add headers
   781вҶ’      sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => c.header));
   782вҶ’      
   783вҶ’      // Add data rows
   784вҶ’      const sheetData = data[sheetDef.dataKey];
   785вҶ’      for (const row of sheetData) {
   786вҶ’        sheet.addRow(sheetDef.columns.map(c => row[c.key]));
   787вҶ’      }
   788вҶ’      
   789вҶ’      // Apply formatting
   790вҶ’      this.applyExcelFormatting(sheet, sheetDef);
   791вҶ’    }
   792вҶ’    
   793вҶ’    const buffer = await workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer();
   794вҶ’    
   795вҶ’    const filename = `${template.name}_${format(new Date(), 'yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')}.xlsx`;
   796вҶ’    const url = await this.storage.upload(filename, buffer);
   797вҶ’    
   798вҶ’    return {
   799вҶ’      filename,
   800вҶ’      url,
   801вҶ’      expiresAt: addDays(new Date(), 7),
   802вҶ’      sizeBytes: buffer.length
   803вҶ’    };
   804вҶ’  }
   805вҶ’}
   806вҶ’```

   979вҶ’
   980вҶ’### 7.10.2 Circuit Breaker
   981вҶ’
   982вҶ’```typescript
   983вҶ’interface CircuitBreakerConfig {
   984вҶ’  failureThreshold: number;    // Failures before opening
   985вҶ’  successThreshold: number;    // Successes before closing
   986вҶ’  timeout: number;             // Time in open state before half-open (ms)
   987вҶ’}
   988вҶ’
   989вҶ’class CircuitBreaker {
   990вҶ’  private state: 'CLOSED' | 'OPEN' | 'HALF_OPEN' = 'CLOSED';
   991вҶ’  private failures = 0;
   992вҶ’  private successes = 0;
   993вҶ’  private lastFailureTime: Date | null = null;
   994вҶ’  
   995вҶ’  async execute<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
   996вҶ’    if (this.state === 'OPEN') {
   997вҶ’      if (Date.now() - this.lastFailureTime!.getTime() > this.config.timeout) {
   998вҶ’        this.state = 'HALF_OPEN';
   999вҶ’      } else {
  1000вҶ’        throw new Error('Circuit breaker is OPEN');
  1001вҶ’      }
  1002вҶ’    }
  1003вҶ’    
  1004вҶ’    try {
  1005вҶ’      const result = await fn();
  1006вҶ’      this.onSuccess();
  1007вҶ’      return result;
  1008вҶ’    } catch (error) {
  1009вҶ’      this.onFailure();
  1010вҶ’      throw error;
  1011вҶ’    }
  1012вҶ’  }
  1013вҶ’  
  1014вҶ’  private onSuccess() {
  1015вҶ’    this.failures = 0;
  1016вҶ’    if (this.state === 'HALF_OPEN') {
  1017вҶ’      this.successes++;
  1018вҶ’      if (this.successes >= this.config.successThreshold) {
  1019вҶ’        this.state = 'CLOSED';
  1020вҶ’        this.successes = 0;
  1021вҶ’      }
  1022вҶ’    }
  1023вҶ’  }
  1024вҶ’  
  1025вҶ’  private onFailure() {
  1026вҶ’    this.failures++;
  1027вҶ’    this.lastFailureTime = new Date();
  1028вҶ’    if (this.failures >= this.config.failureThreshold) {
  1029вҶ’      this.state = 'OPEN';
  1030вҶ’    }
  1031вҶ’  }
  1032вҶ’}
  1033вҶ’```

1вҶ’# Metro Fleet Mileage Management System
     2вҶ’# Part 6: User Interface Specification
     3вҶ’
     4вҶ’**Document Version:** 2.0  
     5вҶ’**Status:** APPROVED FOR DEVELOPMENT
     6вҶ’
     7вҶ’---
     8вҶ’
     9вҶ’## 6.1 Design System Overview
    10вҶ’
    11вҶ’### 6.1.1 Technology Stack
    12вҶ’
    13вҶ’| Component | Technology | Purpose |
    14вҶ’|-----------|------------|---------|
    15вҶ’| Framework | React 18 | Component architecture |
    16вҶ’| State | Zustand | Global state management |
    17вҶ’| Canvas | deck.gl | WebGL timeline rendering |
    18вҶ’| Components | BlueprintJS v6 | UI component library |
    19вҶ’| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Utility-first styling |
    20вҶ’| Build | Vite | Development and bundling |
    21вҶ’| Types | TypeScript | Type safety |
    22вҶ’
    23вҶ’### 6.1.2 Design Principles
    24вҶ’
    25вҶ’1. **Information Density**: Maximize data visibility without overwhelming
    26вҶ’2. **Semantic Zoom**: Data representation adapts to zoom level
    27вҶ’3. **Direct Manipulation**: Drag-and-drop with immediate feedback
    28вҶ’4. **Progressive Disclosure**: Details on demand
    29вҶ’5. **Constraint Visibility**: Show limits, not just violations

   222вҶ’```
   223вҶ’
   224вҶ’### 6.3.5 Hybrid Rendering Architecture
   225вҶ’
   226вҶ’**CRITICAL:** The canvas uses **hybrid rendering** to optimize for each zoom level:
   227вҶ’
   228вҶ’| Zoom Level | Renderer | Rationale |
   229вҶ’|------------|----------|-----------|
   230вҶ’| Strategic (0-4) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Heatmap cells, no text needed |
   231вҶ’| Tactical-Wide (4-8) | **deck.gl (WebGL)** | Compressed blocks, minimal text |
   232вҶ’| Tactical (8-12) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Gantt bars with crisp text labels |
   233вҶ’| Operational (12-16) | **Konva (HTML5 Canvas)** | Detailed cards with readable text |
   234вҶ’| Detail (16-20) | **React DOM** | Rich interactions, form inputs |
   235вҶ’
   236вҶ’**Why Hybrid?**
   237вҶ’
   238вҶ’deck.gl uses WebGL for rendering, which has limitations for text-heavy views:
   239вҶ’- Font atlases blur during zoom operations
   240вҶ’- Hit-testing for drag-and-drop is complex in WebGL
   241вҶ’- Text rendering feels less crisp than native canvas/DOM
   242вҶ’
   243вҶ’By switching to Konva (HTML5 Canvas 2D) at tactical zoom levels, we get:
   244вҶ’- Crisp text rendering at all scales
   245вҶ’- Native canvas hit-testing for smooth drag-and-drop
   246вҶ’- Better performance for hundreds of labeled PM blocks
   247вҶ’
   248вҶ’```typescript
   249вҶ’interface CanvasLayers {
   250вҶ’  // === DECK.GL LAYERS (Strategic/Overview) ===
   251вҶ’  // Used for zoom levels 0-8 where text is minimal
   252вҶ’  
   253вҶ’  strategicHeatmapLayer: HeatmapLayer;    // PM density heatmap
   254вҶ’  timelineAxisLayer: LineLayer;            // Time axis lines
   255вҶ’  quarterBoundaryLayer: LineLayer;         // Quarter separators
   256вҶ’  
   257вҶ’  // === KONVA LAYERS (Tactical/Operational) ===
   258вҶ’  // Used for zoom levels 8-16 where text labels are critical
   259вҶ’  
   260вҶ’  ganttBlockLayer: Konva.Layer;            // PM blocks with labels
   261вҶ’  constraintWallLayer: Konva.Layer;        // Compliance windows
   262вҶ’  trainLabelLayer: Konva.Layer;            // Train number labels
   263вҶ’  tooltipLayer: Konva.Layer;               // Hover tooltips
   264вҶ’  dragPreviewLayer: Konva.Layer;           // Drag-and-drop preview
   265вҶ’  
   266вҶ’  // === REACT DOM OVERLAY (Detail) ===
   267вҶ’  // Used for zoom level 16+ where rich interactions needed
   268вҶ’  
   269вҶ’  detailCardOverlay: React.ReactNode;      // Expanded PM cards
   270вҶ’  editFormOverlay: React.ReactNode;        // Inline editing
   271вҶ’}
   272вҶ’
   273вҶ’// Layer visibility by zoom level
   274вҶ’function getActiveRenderer(zoom: number): 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react' {
   275вҶ’  if (zoom < 8) return 'deckgl';
   276вҶ’  if (zoom < 16) return 'konva';
   277вҶ’  return 'react';
   278вҶ’}
   279вҶ’```
   280вҶ’
   281вҶ’**Transition Handling:**
   282вҶ’
   283вҶ’When crossing renderer boundaries, animate opacity to avoid jarring switches:
   284вҶ’
   285вҶ’```typescript
   286вҶ’function handleZoomChange(prevZoom: number, newZoom: number): void {
   287вҶ’  const prevRenderer = getActiveRenderer(prevZoom);
   288вҶ’  const newRenderer = getActiveRenderer(newZoom);
   289вҶ’  
   290вҶ’  if (prevRenderer !== newRenderer) {
   291вҶ’    // Cross-fade over 200ms
   292вҶ’    animateOpacity(prevRenderer, 1, 0, 200);
   293вҶ’    animateOpacity(newRenderer, 0, 1, 200);
   294вҶ’  }
   295вҶ’}
   296вҶ’```
   297вҶ’
   298вҶ’### 6.3.6 Zoom and Pan Controls
   299вҶ’
   300вҶ’```typescript
   301вҶ’interface CanvasViewState {
   302вҶ’  // Viewport
   303вҶ’  longitude: number;    // Time position (days from epoch)
   304вҶ’  latitude: number;     // Train index position
   305вҶ’  zoom: number;         // Zoom level (0-20)
   306вҶ’  
   307вҶ’  // Derived
   308вҶ’  timeRange: {
   309вҶ’    start: Date;
   310вҶ’    end: Date;
   311вҶ’  };
   312вҶ’  visibleTrains: string[];
   313вҶ’  zoomLevel: 'STRATEGIC' | 'TACTICAL_WIDE' | 'TACTICAL' | 'OPERATIONAL' | 'DETAIL';
   314вҶ’  activeRenderer: 'deckgl' | 'konva' | 'react';  // Current rendering mode
   315вҶ’}
   316вҶ’
   317вҶ’// Zoom level thresholds
   318вҶ’const ZOOM_THRESHOLDS = {
   319вҶ’  STRATEGIC: { min: 0, max: 4, renderer: 'deckgl' },
   320вҶ’  TACTICAL_WIDE: { min: 4, max: 8, renderer: 'deckgl' },
   321вҶ’  TACTICAL: { min: 8, max: 12, renderer: 'konva' },
   322вҶ’  OPERATIONAL: { min: 12, max: 16, renderer: 'konva' },
   323вҶ’  DETAIL: { min: 16, max: 20, renderer: 'react' }
   324вҶ’};
   325вҶ’
   326вҶ’// Keyboard shortcuts
   327вҶ’const KEYBOARD_SHORTCUTS = {
   328вҶ’  '+': 'Zoom in',
   329вҶ’  '-': 'Zoom out',
   330вҶ’  't': 'Go to today',
   331вҶ’  's': 'Strategic view',
   332вҶ’  'o': 'Operational view',
   333вҶ’  'Escape': 'Deselect',
   334вҶ’  'Delete': 'Cancel selected PM (with confirmation)',
   335вҶ’  'l': 'Lock/unlock selected PM'
   336вҶ’};
   337вҶ’```
   338вҶ’
   339вҶ’---
   340вҶ’
   341вҶ’## 6.4 Drag-and-Drop Interaction
   342вҶ’
   343вҶ’### 6.4.1 PM Rescheduling Flow
   344вҶ’
   345вҶ’```
   346вҶ’User Action                     System Response
   347вҶ’ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬ГўвҖқвӮ¬
   348вҶ’1. Hover over PM block         ГўвҖ вҖҷ Cursor changes to grab
   349вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Block highlights
   350вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Tooltip shows PM details
   351вҶ’
   352вҶ’2. Mouse down on PM block      ГўвҖ вҖҷ Block enters "dragging" state
   353вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Constraint walls become prominent
   354вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Other PMs fade slightly
   355вҶ’
   356вҶ’3. Drag PM to new position     ГўвҖ вҖҷ Ghost preview follows cursor
   357вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Real-time validation:
   358вҶ’                                 - Green outline = valid drop
   359вҶ’                                 - Red outline = constraint violation
   360вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Violation tooltip explains issue

   362вҶ’4. Release over valid position ГўвҖ вҖҷ Confirmation dialog:

   366вҶ’
   367вҶ’5. User confirms               ГўвҖ вҖҷ API call to update PM
   368вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ WebSocket broadcasts change
   369вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ All connected clients update
   370вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ History entry created
   371вҶ’                               ГўвҖ вҖҷ Success toast notification

content.text:
Based on my comprehensive review of all the specification documents, here is a concise user-focused summary:

---

## Metro Fleet Mileage Management System (MMS) - User Summary

**Purpose & Core Functionality**

MMS is a constraint-based fleet planning platform that automates train deployment and maintenance scheduling over a 12-year government contract. It replaces manual Excel-based planning with integrated daily mileage tracking, predictive scheduling, and cost forecasting while preventing "bow wave" accumulation of deferred maintenance.

**Key User-Facing Features**

- **Daily Mileage Ingestion**: Automatic processing of CSV uploads with anomaly detection
- **Run Assignment Optimization**: AI-driven allocation of 128 trains to routes, balancing workload and mileage across the fleet
- **Preventive Maintenance (PM) Scheduling**: Automated planning of C1/C2/C3/C4 inspections and overhauls with compliance window constraints
- **Strategic Planning**: 1-10 year capacity projections with material and labor forecasting to prevent maintenance backlogs
- **Scenario Simulation**: What-if analysis for testing alternative plans before committing
- **Baseline Publishing**: Immutable snapshots for drift tracking and long-term plan stability
- **Interactive Timeline Canvas**: Zoomable interface from 12-year heatmaps down to daily detail cards using semantic zoom

**Domain Concepts**

- **PM Types**: C1-C4 maintenance cycles + major overhauls, each with compliance windows (earliest-to-latest-allowed dates)
- **Soft Constraints**: Preferences (mileage balance, labor leveling) that can be violated with cost penalties
- **Hard Constraints**: Safety/physical limits that must never be violated (e.g., trains grounded by corrective work)
- **Material Constraints**: Tracks spare parts availability per depot; blocks PM scheduling when critical parts are unavailable
- **Service Demand**: Minimum fleet availability targets (e.g., "need 110 trains operational on weekdays")
- **Drift**: Deviation from published baseline; triggers replanning when >5%

**Integration Points**

- **Maximo CMMS**: Bidirectional sync for work orders; receives unavailability events (corrective maintenance status) to prevent scheduling trains blocked by repairs
- **Inventory System**: Daily snapshots of parts availability by location; used to validate PM feasibility
- **Operations/OCC**: Export optimized run assignments; receive actual mileage feedback
- **Email Notifications**: Alerts for drift, material shortages, overload warnings, baseline publications

**UI Capabilities**

- **Infinite Canvas** (deck.gl): Single view spanning full 12-year horizon with context-dependent detail
- **Semantic Zoom**: Representation adaptsвҖ”strategic view shows PM density heatmaps; tactical zooms show Gantt bars; operational zoom shows full detail cards with crew/materials
- **Drag-and-Drop Rescheduling**: Real-time constraint validation (green=valid, red=violation) with immediate feedback
- **Baseline/Drift Visualization**: Side-by-side overlay showing planned vs. actual; highlights trains needing attention
- **Man-hour Overlay**: Weekly workload bars showing utilization and adjustment suggestions
- **Constraint Walls**: Visual boundaries marking earliest/latest allowed PM dates; prevents manual violations
- **Explanation Traces**: Every optimization recommendation includes human-readable justification factors

**Operational Reality**

The system prioritizes reliability over optimization: when Maximo reports a train in corrective maintenance, MMS enforces edit locks and prevents that train from being scheduled. Planners always see the "why" behind decisions and can override recommendations with audit trails. Long-term plans use published baselines to prevent jittery forecastsвҖ”strategic recalculation only occurs when explicitly triggered.

Human operators remain in control; the system provides guidance and automation but requires approval for material changes.
